channel.title: Bob's Article Podcasts
channel.description: Articles Bob flagged, narrated as audio and delivered automatically.
channel.itunes:author: Bob Erdman
channel.itunes:summary: Articles Bob flagged, narrated as audio and delivered automatically.

item.title: The Employee's Guide to Working Alongside AI
item.description: A practical guide for employees navigating AI in the workplace: naming the fear honestly, understanding what AI does well and badly, and concrete moves to stay valuable.

item.title: Why AI Pilots Succeed and AI Deployments Fail
item.description: Steven Hawk explains why AI pilots routinely beat the deployments that follow, naming the selection effects and organizational gaps that derail production systems and the practices, like designing deployment first and assigning real ownership, that bridge them.

item.title: AI and the Board: Governing Technology You Don't Fully Understand
item.description: Steven Hawk argues boards can govern AI without technical mastery, diagnosing common oversight failures and offering the accountability structures, risk-appetite framing, and pointed questions directors need to provide real oversight.

item.title: The AI Strategy That Actually Ships
item.description: Steven Hawk argues most AI failures are execution problems disguised as strategy problems, and lays out how organizations escape the planning trap by shipping small, building feedback loops, and treating strategy as a learning process.

item.title: Build, Buy, or Borrow: The Real AI Decision Most Companies Are Getting Wrong
item.description: Steven Hawk breaks down the build-buy-borrow decision for sourcing AI capability, detailing the hidden costs of each path and the honest conditions and recurring questions that should drive the choice.

item.title: Trust as Infrastructure: Why AI Ethics Is a Business Problem, Not a Philosophy Problem
item.description: Steven Hawk reframes AI ethics from a philosophy exercise into an operational requirement, arguing trust is infrastructure that determines adoption, latitude, and talent, and must be built, maintained, and governed with teeth.

item.title: What AI Gets Wrong About People (And What That Means for the Humans Building It)
item.description: The final piece in Steven Hawk's AI series argues that AI's deepest limitations are not technical but human: it confuses expression with experience, mistakes consistency for truth, and misses tacit knowledge and meaning, with implications for the people building it.

item.title: The Questions Every Executive Should Ask Before Approving an AI Project
item.description: Twenty questions across six domains—problem, solution, data, risk, organization, and investment—that executives can use to rigorously evaluate AI proposals without being technologists.

item.title: When to Say No to AI
item.description: A framework of six questions to decide when not to deploy AI, and how to make the case for restraint.

item.title: Talking to Humans About AI: A Translation Guide (sample)
item.description: A narrated sample of Steven Hawk's guide to communicating about AI across an organization. Pilot episode validating the feed.

item.title: The AI Glossary Nobody Asked For (But Everyone Needs)
item.description: A plain-language reference defining 30-plus AI terms for non-engineers, grouped by theme, each with what it means, what it does not mean, and why it matters.
